name: phase05_drag_and_drop
outcome_goal: Drag modules between cells, add from palette, resize spans

jobs.label: p5_dnd_grid
jobs.prompt:
You are working in /Users/nate/Praxis/Code&DBs/Workflow/surfaces/app/src/.

Add HTML5 drag-and-drop to QuadrantGrid.tsx so users can rearrange modules.

The QuadrantGrid already has:
- useManifestOverlay hook that reads from World 'ui.layout.quadrants'
- world.propose() for optimistic state updates
- gear icon on each cell

Add drag-and-drop:
1. Each quadrant-cell with a module gets draggable={true}
2. onDragStart: store the source quadrantId in dataTransfer
3. onDragOver on all cells (including empty): preventDefault to allow drop
4. onDrop: read source quadrantId from dataTransfer
   - If dropping on an occupied cell: SWAP the two modules (exchange their module/span/config in World)
   - If dropping on an empty cell: MOVE the module (remove from source, add to target)
   - Write changes to World via world.propose('ui.layout.quadrants', updatedQuadrants)
5. Visual feedback: onDragOver adds a highlight class, onDragLeave removes it

CSS additions to QuadrantGrid.css:
- .quadrant-cell[draggable] { cursor: grab }
- .quadrant-cell.drag-over { border-color: var(--accent); background: rgba(88,166,255,0.05) }
- .quadrant-cell:active { cursor: grabbing }

Do NOT add any npm packages. Use native HTML5 drag-and-drop only.
Do NOT break the gear icon, config editor, or error boundary.

Write the updated files.

jobs.label: p5_module_palette
jobs.prompt:
You are working in /Users/nate/Praxis/Code&DBs/Workflow/surfaces/app/src/.

Create src/grid/ModulePalette.tsx — a sidebar panel listing all registered modules that can be dragged onto empty grid cells.

Behavior:
- Triggered by a '+' button in the QuadrantGrid header area
- Shows a slide-out panel on the right side (position fixed, width 280px, full height)
- Lists all modules from listModules() grouped by type (display, input, tool, composite)
- Each module card shows: name, type badge, default span
- Module cards are draggable (HTML5 native, same as grid drag)
- onDragStart sets dataTransfer with {action: 'add', module: id, span: defaultSpan}
- QuadrantGrid's onDrop on empty cells should handle this 'add' action
- Close button and clicking outside closes the palette

Styling:
- Dark theme: bg var(--bg), border-left 1px solid var(--border)
- Type badges: display=blue, input=green, tool=purple, composite=orange
- Smooth slide-in transition

Also create src/grid/ModulePalette.css

Update QuadrantGrid.tsx to:
- Import ModulePalette
- Add state: showPalette (boolean)
- Add '+' button next to the title
- Render ModulePalette when showPalette is true
- Handle the 'add' action in the onDrop handler

Write all files.
